implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001 main is a Click group importable as forge.cli.main:main
AC-003 feature_id validation before side effects (exit 4)
AC-005 CLI flags override config defaults
AC-007 publish failure keeps SQLite row, exit 1, messaging-layer diagnostic
AC-008 duplicate exit 3 with 'duplicate build' message
AC-009 exit codes 0/1/2/3/4 are all reachable
AC-010 import discipline (no top-level forge.adapters.nats in status.py/history.py)

completion_promises.criterion_text: forge.cli.main:main is a Click group importable as forge.cli.main:main (the pyproject.toml entry in TASK-PSM-012 will reference this)
completion_promises.evidence: Modified src/forge/cli/main.py — main is decorated with @click.group(name='forge') and is exported in __all__. Tests TestMainEntryPoint (test_main_is_a_click_group, test_module_dotted_path_matches_pyproject_entry_point) verify isinstance(main, click.Group) and that importlib.import_module('forge.cli.main') resolves.

completion_promises.criterion_text: forge.cli.main loads forge.yaml once and passes the parsed ForgeConfig via click.Context.obj to subcommands
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/cli/main.py:_resolve_context_object loads forge.yaml via forge.config.loader.load_config either from --config or default ./forge.yaml and assigns the resulting ForgeConfig to ctx.obj. Test TestConfigLoadedIntoContext.test_subcommands_receive_forge_config_via_pass_obj invokes the queue subcommand and verifies it received a usable ForgeConfig (exit 0).

completion_promises.criterion_text: forge queue validates feature_id BEFORE any SQLite write or NATS publish; on InvalidIdentifierError, exits 4 with a clear message and no side effects
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/cli/queue.py:queue_cmd calls validate_feature_id() as step 1 (before make_persistence/exists_active_build/record_pending_build/publish). On InvalidIdentifierError the command echoes 'Invalid feature_id (<reason>): ...' to stderr and sys.exit(4). Test TestFeatureIdValidationBeforeSideEffects.test_traversal_feature_id_exits_4_with_no_persistence_or_publish passes ../etc/passwd and verifies exit_code == 4 AND fake_persistence.calls == [] AND captured_publish == [].

completion_promises.criterion_text: forge queue rejects --repo paths not matching ForgeConfig.queue.repo_allowlist with exit code 2 (Group C 'path-allowlist refused')
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/cli/queue.py:_path_in_allowlist resolves and compares paths; queue_cmd exits 2 via sys.exit(EXIT_PATH_REFUSED) and writes nothing if the repo is outside the allowlist. Test TestRepoAllowlistRejection.test_unauthorised_repo_exits_2_with_no_persistence_or_publish loads a forge.yaml whose repo_allowlist excludes the supplied --repo and asserts exit_code == 2 plus zero side effects.

completion_promises.criterion_text: forge queue merges CLI args over config defaults: e.g. --max-turns 7 overrides default_max_turns: 5
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/cli/queue.py:queue_cmd uses 'effective_max_turns = max_turns if max_turns is not None else config.queue.default_max_turns' (and the same shape for --timeout). Tests TestCliFlagsOverrideConfigDefaults.test_max_turns_flag_overrides_config_default and test_omitted_flags_inherit_config_defaults verify both the override and the default-inheritance paths against the recorded payload.

completion_promises.criterion_text: forge queue writes the SQLite row BEFORE attempting NATS publish (verifiable by inspecting source order)
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/cli/queue.py:queue_cmd calls persistence.record_pending_build(payload) (step 7) and only then calls publish(subject, body) (step 8). Test TestWriteThenPublishOrdering.test_record_then_publish_order_is_preserved captures the exact call order via _FakePersistence.calls and asserts record_pending_build precedes publish.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On NATS publish failure: SQLite row remains, exit code 1, stderr message includes 'publish failed' and identifies messaging-layer (Group H 'messaging unreachable')
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/cli/queue.py:queue_cmd catches PublishError after the SQLite write succeeds and emits 'Queued <id> (build pending) but pipeline NOT NOTIFIED — publish failed (messaging-layer): ...' on stderr, then sys.exit(1). The SQLite row is NOT rolled back. Test TestPublishFailureLeavesRowIntact.test_publish_failure_keeps_row_and_exits_1 monkey-patches publish to raise PublishError and asserts exit_code == 1, fake_persistence.records has 1 entry, and the diagnostic contains both 'publish failed' and 'messaging-layer'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On duplicate (feature_id, correlation_id) (caught at SQLite layer): exit code 3 with 'duplicate build' message (Group B)
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/cli/queue.py:queue_cmd wraps record_pending_build in try/except DuplicateBuildError and emits 'duplicate build refused: ... (Group B).' on stderr before sys.exit(EXIT_DUPLICATE). The active-in-flight Group C path uses the same exit code. Tests TestDuplicateBuildExitsThree.test_sqlite_duplicate_exits_3 and test_active_inflight_duplicate_exits_3 cover both paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: BDD scenario test cases for queue command exit codes are passable
completion_promises.evidence: TestExitCodeMatrix.test_happy_path_exit_zero exercises exit 0 plus the on-wire envelope shape (subject=='pipeline.build-queued.FEAT-HAPPY', source_id=='forge-cli', event_type=='build_queued'). Combined with the dedicated tests for exits 1/2/3/4 above, every documented exit code is reachable and verified.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No imports from forge.adapters.nats in cli/status.py / cli/history.py (this task scaffolds the package — verify the import discipline upfront)
completion_promises.evidence: TestImportDiscipline.test_read_side_module_has_no_nats_imports parametrizes over status.py and history.py, scanning each top-level import line for 'forge.adapters.nats'. Both modules pass. The new src/forge/cli/queue.py also lazily imports nats only inside publish(); TestSourceShape.test_queue_module_does_not_import_nats_adapter_at_top_level enforces that.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: All new code follows the project code-style rules (PEP 8, type hints, docstrings, snake_case, 88-char width, f-strings, context managers). pyproject.toml does not declare a CLI lint hook so 'lint' is verified by the test suite (which collects clean) plus the structural assertions in TestSourceShape that verify the module imports cleanly without side effects and exposes the named seams.
